title: This Fish Can Leap Out of the Water to Shock You
script: This fish generates more voltage than a wall outlet, enough to drop a horse. The electric eel. It is not actually an eel, it is a knife fish that builds up to six hundred volts in organs running the length of its body, then fires it in pulses to stun prey before it can react. Threatened on land, it does something stranger: it leaps out of the water and presses its chin against the target to deliver the shock directly, first filmed by scientists in 2016. A jolt like that will not kill a healthy adult, but enough to knock a grown man underwater. Why would a fish ever climb out of the water to attack?
